meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade SaaS products rapidly.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth for founders. By leveraging AI-driven Git workflows, you can build, document, and deploy production-ready features in hours, not weeks. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, turning every commit into a verifiable proof of technical capability and revenue potential.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the founder's greatest enemy. You have the vision, the market insight, and the drive, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of abandoned tutorials or, worse, an empty void. You fear that without a 'proven' track record, investors and customers will dismiss your SaaS before you even launch.

This is the turning point. We are moving beyond manual coding into an era of AI-orchestrated development. By integrating Git with AI agents like Claude Code and architectural tools like Antigravity, you can transform your raw ideas into a high-fidelity, commit-rich portfolio that screams 'technical authority'—all while building your actual business.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Define the SaaS core to ensure your repo structure is industry-standard from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS boilerplate using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Organize by feature-first architecture and include a comprehensive README.md.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --setup 'init project structure with clean architecture patterns'

steps.title: Automated Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build your MVP features iteratively. Each feature should be a distinct branch, demonstrating a clear development lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a user authentication module with secure JWT handling and a persistent database schema for user profiles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement user authentication with email/password and social providers'

steps.title: Commit History Engineering
steps.description: Ensure your Git history tells a story of progress. Use AI to write clean, descriptive commit messages that document your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current diff and write a conventional commit message that highlights the business value of these changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat(auth): implement secure session management and database persistence'

steps.title: Documentation as Proof
steps.description: Generate technical documentation that explains the 'why' behind your code, proving you understand system design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical design document for the current codebase, focusing on security, scalability, and API integration paths.
steps.claude_code_command: claude docs --generate 'create comprehensive system design documentation for the current repo'

steps.title: Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Deploy to a live URL. Nothing validates a portfolio faster than a working product that people can click on.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline for automated deployment to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.

pro_tips: Treat your Git history as a marketing asset; high-quality commit messages are more important than the code itself for recruiters and partners.
Use Claude Code to perform security audits on your own code; it demonstrates you prioritize production stability over quick hacks.
Always include a 'Business Logic' section in your README to show you are building products, not just writing scripts.
Automate your testing suite; an empty repo with 90% test coverage is more impressive than a massive repo with zero tests.
Publicize your progress by linking your commits to a Trello or Notion board; transparency builds trust in your execution capability.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is live, pick one core feature, refine it with user feedback, and move to your second SaaS project to show repeat execution.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to turn your lack of experience into a competitive advantage. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Build, deploy, and scale your vision today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
